title: cronsplain bootstrap+contract: package.json + lib/errors.js + lib/fields.js + tests/test_fields.js (SOLE ROOT, F1 shared infra)
description: SOLE ROOT of the cronsplain epic (projects/cronsplain/, Node.js, NON-Python). Owns every shared-infra file (F1): the module-system contract (package.json), the shared error type (lib/errors.js), and the shared field metadata (lib/fields.js) that the parser, explain, and schedule all require(). Node has NO test package-marker (node --test discovers by filename glob), so package.json is the analogous shared-infra file and must have exactly one owner. All confirmed decisions (Q1-Q4, 2026-07-05) are baked into the SHARED CONVENTIONS acceptance item. Execute from this task JSON alone.
acceptance_criteria: OWNS EXACTLY (create only these; touch no other file): projects/cronsplain/package.json, projects/cronsplain/lib/errors.js, projects/cronsplain/lib/fields.js, projects/cronsplain/tests/test_fields.js.

package.json: {"name":"cronsplain","version":"1.0.0","private":true,"bin":{"cronsplain":"bin/cronsplain.js"},"scripts":{"test":"node --test"},"engines":{"node":">=18"}}. NO "type" field (=> CommonJS default). NO dependencies and NO devDependencies (ZERO npm deps; node core + node:test only).

lib/errors.js: export a CronParseError class extending Error (this.name='CronParseError'; constructor(message, {expression, field}={}) storing this.expression and this.field). module.exports = { CronParseError }. This is the ONLY error type the parser throws and the CLI catches for clean messages.

lib/fields.js: export the field metadata consumed by parser/explain/schedule. FIELDS: ordered array [minute,hour,dayOfMonth,month,dayOfWeek], each {name, min, max, display} with bounds minute 0-59, hour 0-23, dayOfMonth 1-31, month 1-12, dayOfWeek 0-7. MONTH_NAMES: case-insensitive map JAN..DEC -> 1..12. DOW_NAMES: case-insensitive map SUN..SAT -> 0..6. normalizeDow(n): 7 -> 0. MONTH_DISPLAY: ['January'..'December']; DOW_DISPLAY: ['Sunday'..'Saturday'] (for explain). module.exports the constants + helpers.

tests/test_fields.js: node:test suite (const {test}=require('node:test'); const assert=require('node:assert')) asserting bounds per field; MONTH_NAMES case-insensitive (JAN=1, dec=12); DOW_NAMES (SUN=0, sat=6); normalizeDow(7)===0; FIELDS length 5 and order.

SHARED CONVENTIONS (ALL cronsplain tasks; confirmed 2026-07-05): CommonJS (require/module.exports, NO "type":"module"); ZERO npm deps (node core + node:test only); project root projects/cronsplain/; run tests from that dir with `node --test` (or `node --test tests/test_fields.js`). Grammar (Q1): 5 fields minute/hour/dayOfMonth/month/dayOfWeek; syntaxes value, range a-b, step */n and a-b/n (n>=1), comma list, names JAN-DEC (month) & SUN-SAT (dow) case-insensitive, wildcard *; dow 0-7 with 7==0. Vixie extensions (@daily/@reboot macros, L, W, #, ?, bare a/n step) are OUT -> throw CronParseError. DOM/DOW coupling (Q2): when BOTH dayOfMonth and dayOfWeek are restricted (neither *), a day matches if EITHER matches (OR/union); if exactly one is *, only the other constrains; if both *, every day. next semantics (Q3): strictly AFTER --from (exclusive), minute-aligned (seconds=0, ms=0), ascending UTC; --count default 5; --from default now(). Timezone (Q4/tz): all matching + output in UTC; naive ISO --from (no offset/Z) interpreted as UTC; offset/Z respected then converted; output ISO-8601 with trailing Z.

HARNESS PROTOCOL: claim `python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py claim T-044 --agent <you>`; lock each owned file `python3 .harness/bin/lock.py acquire <path> --holder <you> --task T-044`; announce `blackboard.py update T-044 --status in_progress --agent <you> --note ...`; hand off `blackboard.py handoff T-044 --to-role verifier --agent <you> --note <replayable evidence>`; NEVER self-mark done; release locks when done.

VERIFY (put the exact commands + output in the handoff note): from projects/cronsplain/ run `node --test tests/test_fields.js` (green) and `node -e "require('./lib/errors'); require('./lib/fields')"` (no throw).

notes.note: plan: create projects/cronsplain/{package.json,lib/errors.js,lib/fields.js,tests/test_fields.js} per acceptance criteria (CommonJS, zero deps, node:test); verify with node --test tests/test_fields.js

EXPECTED: 4 owned files created exactly as specified, node --test tests/test_fields.js green, require('./lib/errors')+require('./lib/fields') no-throw. ACTUAL: matches exactly, 11/11 tests pass, exit 0 on both verify commands. TAXONOMY: OPEN-QUESTION (F1-style friction, precedent mdtoc tests/__init__.py P-013) — node's default 'node --test' / 'npm test' recursive glob discovery does NOT pick up files named tests/test_*.js (prefix style, as fixed by this epic's acceptance criteria for test_fields.js and all downstream test_parser.js/test_explain.js/test_cli.js/test_integration.js). Empirically verified: node auto-discovers (a) ANY filename inside a dir literally named 'test' (singular), or (b) files under 'tests' (plural) ONLY if basename ends in .test.js/-test.js/_test.js. A prefix-style 'test_fields.js' under 'tests/' (plural) is invoked correctly ONLY via an explicit path arg (node --test tests/test_fields.js -> 11 pass) or explicit shell glob (node --test tests/*.js); bare 'node --test' / 'npm test' from the project root silently reports 0 tests, 0 pass, exit 0 (a false-green, not a failure) since no file matched. package.json scripts.test is fixed verbatim by T-044 acceptance criteria as "node --test" (no path) so I did NOT change it. RECOMMENDATION for T-045/T-046/T-050/T-051/T-052 and the final-join verifier: always invoke node --test with explicit paths, e.g. node --test tests/*.js (shell-expanded) from projects/cronsplain/, never rely on bare 'node --test'/'npm test' to prove suite is green.

HANDOFF -> verifier: REPLAY: cd projects/cronsplain && node --test tests/test_fields.js  =>  observed: 11 tests, 11 pass, 0 fail, exit 0. ALSO: cd projects/cronsplain && node -e "require('./lib/errors'); require('./lib/fields')"  =>  observed: no output, exit 0 (no throw). ALSO ran via bounded loop: python3 .harness/bin/goal_mode.py run --task T-044 --cmd 'cd projects/cronsplain && node --test tests/test_fields.js' --timeout 300 => 'GOAL REACHED ... on iteration 1/3'. Files created (owns exactly, touched nothing else): projects/cronsplain/package.json (name/version/private/bin/scripts.test=node --test/engines>=18; NO type field => CommonJS default; NO dependencies/devDependencies => zero npm deps -- verified via node -e reading package.json, both checks pass), projects/cronsplain/lib/errors.js (module.exports={CronParseError}; class extends Error, name='CronParseError', constructor(message,{expression,field}={}) stores this.expression/this.field, Error.captureStackTrace guarded), projects/cronsplain/lib/fields.js (module.exports={FIELDS,MONTH_NAMES,DOW_NAMES,MONTH_DISPLAY,DOW_DISPLAY,normalizeDow}; FIELDS is the 5-entry ordered array minute/hour/dayOfMonth/month/dayOfWeek with exact bounds min/max plus a display string; MONTH_NAMES/DOW_NAMES are Proxy-backed case-insensitive maps built via an internal makeCaseInsensitiveMap helper (get/has/ownKeys/getOwnPropertyDescriptor all upper-case the lookup key) so MONTH_NAMES.jan===MONTH_NAMES.JAN===1 and 'dec' in MONTH_NAMES is true; normalizeDow(7)===0, all other n unchanged), projects/cronsplain/tests/test_fields.js (node:test + node:assert, 11 tests: FIELDS length/order, FIELDS bounds per field, FIELDS display strings, MONTH_NAMES full JAN-DEC + case-insensitivity incl. 'dec'->12, DOW_NAMES full SUN-SAT + case-insensitivity, normalizeDow(7)/other values, MONTH_DISPLAY/DOW_DISPLAY length+endpoints, round-trip checks tying names to display arrays). DEVIATION/RISK flagged as OPEN-QUESTION in the T-044 task notes (taxonomy-prefixed): node's default 'node --test'/'npm test' recursive discovery does NOT pick up 'tests/test_*.js' prefix-named files (only *.test.js/*-test.js/*_test.js suffix-named files under a 'tests' dir, or ANY filename under a dir literally named 'test' singular, are auto-discovered) -- verified empirically in a throwaway scratch probe (node v24.15.0): bare 'node --test' from projects/cronsplain/ reports 0 tests / exit 0 (silent false-green), while 'node --test tests/test_fields.js' or 'node --test tests/*.js' correctly discovers and runs it. package.json scripts.test is fixed verbatim by this task's acceptance criteria as 'node --test' so it was NOT altered. Downstream tasks T-045/T-046/T-050/T-051 and the T-052 final-join verifier should invoke node --test with explicit paths (e.g. 'node --test tests/*.js' from projects/cronsplain/) rather than trust bare 'node --test'/'npm test' to prove the suite is green -- see full note text on T-044 for detail. Locks released after this handoff.

VERIFY START: replaying node --test tests/test_fields.js, require() surface, hostile probes (CronParseError instanceof, Proxy map weird keys, FIELDS immutability, cross-cwd require), and ruling on the bare 'node --test' false-green open-question.

REJECTED (verifier-c, node v24.15.0). SINGLE blocking defect: package.json scripts.test="node --test" is a SILENT FALSE-GREEN. REPLAYED from projects/cronsplain/: bare 'node --test' => 'tests 0 / pass 0 / fail 0', exit 0; 'npm test' (runs scripts.test) => same 0/0/0, exit 0. This lands a false-green in the canonical test command that T-045..T-052 inherit blind (a downstream author or the T-052 final-join running 'npm test' sees green on ZERO discovered tests). EMPIRICAL RULING on the open-question the worker escalated: option (i) 'node --test tests/' FAILS on v24 (Cannot find module .../tests, fail 1, exit 1 -- v24 treats the positional as a module path, NOT a dir glob); option (ii) 'node --test tests/*.js' WORKS three ways -- direct=11 pass/exit0, via sh -c (npm's script shell, glob shell-expanded)=11 pass/exit0, AND node-native glob 'node --test "tests/*.js"' (quoted, shell does NOT expand)=11 pass/exit0. EXACT ONE-LINE FIX (owned file): set package.json scripts.test to "node --test tests/*.js". This is a verifier-blessed DOCUMENTED deviation from acceptance criterion #2's verbatim 'node --test' -- criterion #2's pinned value IS the defect; fix it and cite this note. Everything else PASSED and needs NO change: 11/11 tests green via explicit path; CronParseError instanceof+expression/field preserved through throw/catch; Proxy maps handle 'jAn'(=1),''/'JANUARY'/'JAN '/13/toString/constructor/__proto__/Symbol/number-key all safely (undefined,no crash,no proto-pollution via Object.create(null)); zero deps confirmed at code level (only node:test/node:assert/../lib/fields require'd); cross-cwd require works from repo root, project dir, and /tmp. ADVISORY (non-blocking): FIELDS/MONTH_DISPLAY/DOW_DISPLAY are NOT frozen (FIELDS[0].max mutable, FIELDS.push works) -- shared module cache means one consumer can mutate for all; not required by criteria, but consider Object.freeze in a follow-up. Re-handoff after the one-line scripts.test change; re-verify = 'cd projects/cronsplain && npm test' must show 11 pass.

plan: apply verifier-c's blessed fix - package.json scripts.test -> 'node --test tests/*.js' (fixes the bare node --test / npm test false-green 0/0/0). ADVISORY: also applying Object.freeze hardening to lib/fields.js (FIELDS array+entries, MONTH_DISPLAY, DOW_DISPLAY, and the Proxy-backed name-map targets) per verifier-c's non-blocking recommendation; adding freeze-assertion tests to tests/test_fields.js. Re-verify: cd projects/cronsplain && npm test must show 11 pass (not 0/0/0).

FIX APPLIED (verifier-c blessed deviation, cited): package.json scripts.test changed from the acceptance criterion's verbatim "node --test" to "node --test tests/*.js" -- verifier-c (2026-07-05T04:37:29Z note) empirically proved on node v24.15.0 that option (i) 'node --test tests/' FAILS (Cannot find module .../tests, exit 1) while option (ii) 'node --test tests/*.js' PASSES 3 ways (direct shell glob, npm's sh -c script shell, and node-native quoted glob) -- all verified again here, see BEFORE/AFTER below. This is a documented, verifier-authorized deviation from acceptance criterion #2's pinned scripts.test string; criterion #2's literal value was itself the defect. BEFORE (from projects/cronsplain/): 'npm test' -> tests 0/pass 0/fail 0 exit 0 (silent false-green); 'node --test' (bare) -> tests 0/pass 0/fail 0 exit 0. AFTER: 'npm test' -> tests 14/pass 14/fail 0 exit 0; 'node --test tests/*.js' -> tests 14/pass 14/fail 0 exit 0; 'node --test "tests/*.js"' (quoted, node-native glob) -> tests 14/pass 14/fail 0 exit 0. ADDITIVE HARDENING applied per verifier-c's non-blocking advisory (DEVIATION: additive hardening per verifier advisory, not required by acceptance criteria): lib/fields.js now Object.freeze()s the Proxy target ('store') inside makeCaseInsensitiveMap before wrapping (so MONTH_NAMES/DOW_NAMES mutation attempts throw TypeError in strict mode instead of corrupting silently), Object.freeze()s every FIELDS entry plus the FIELDS array itself, and Object.freeze()s MONTH_DISPLAY and DOW_DISPLAY. tests/test_fields.js gained 3 new tests (11 -> 14 total) asserting Object.isFrozen() on FIELDS/entries/MONTH_DISPLAY/DOW_DISPLAY and that mutation attempts on FIELDS, MONTH_DISPLAY, DOW_DISPLAY, MONTH_NAMES, and DOW_NAMES all throw TypeError and leave values unchanged. Full suite: node --test tests/*.js -> 14 tests, 14 pass, 0 fail, exit 0 (verified 3x with different invocations, plus via bounded loop goal_mode.py run --task T-044 --cmd 'cd projects/cronsplain && npm test' -> GOAL REACHED iteration 1/3). require sanity unchanged: node -e "require('./lib/errors'); require('./lib/fields')" -> exit 0, no throw. Cross-cwd require from repo root also re-verified OK. Files touched this round (all previously-owned, correctly re-locked): projects/cronsplain/package.json, projects/cronsplain/lib/fields.js, projects/cronsplain/tests/test_fields.js (lib/errors.js untouched).

HANDOFF -> verifier: RE-HANDOFF after verifier-c REJECT fixed. REPLAY commands + observed output: (1) cd projects/cronsplain && npm test => 'tests 14 / pass 14 / fail 0', exit 0 (was 0/0/0 before fix -- false-green CONFIRMED GONE). (2) cd projects/cronsplain && node --test tests/*.js => same 14/14/0, exit 0. (3) cd projects/cronsplain && node --test "tests/*.js" (quoted/node-native glob) => same 14/14/0, exit 0. (4) cd projects/cronsplain && node -e "require('./lib/errors'); require('./lib/fields')" => exit 0, no throw. (5) python3 .harness/bin/goal_mode.py run --task T-044 --cmd 'cd projects/cronsplain && npm test' --timeout 300 => 'GOAL REACHED ... on iteration 1/3'. MANDATORY FIX: package.json scripts.test = "node --test tests/*.js" (was "node --test"). Cites verifier-c's 2026-07-05T04:37:29Z reject note as authorization for this deviation from acceptance criterion #2's verbatim pinned value -- verifier-c empirically proved 'node --test tests/' fails (exit 1, module-not-found) and 'node --test tests/*.js' passes 3 ways; I re-verified all 3 ways again above. RECOMMENDED HARDENING APPLIED (non-blocking advisory, disclosed as DEVIATION: additive hardening per verifier advisory): lib/fields.js -- Object.freeze on the Proxy-backed name-map targets (MONTH_NAMES/DOW_NAMES store, frozen before Proxy-wrapping), Object.freeze on each FIELDS entry + the FIELDS array, Object.freeze on MONTH_DISPLAY and DOW_DISPLAY. tests/test_fields.js -- added 3 tests (11->14 total) asserting Object.isFrozen(...) and that mutation attempts on FIELDS/MONTH_DISPLAY/DOW_DISPLAY/MONTH_NAMES/DOW_NAMES all throw TypeError (strict mode) and never stick. Exported API surface UNCHANGED: lib/errors.js module.exports={CronParseError} (untouched this round); lib/fields.js module.exports={FIELDS,MONTH_NAMES,DOW_NAMES,MONTH_DISPLAY,DOW_DISPLAY,normalizeDow} (same names/shapes, now frozen). package.json still: CommonJS (no type field), zero deps (no dependencies/devDependencies), bin.cronsplain=bin/cronsplain.js, engines.node>=18 -- only scripts.test changed. Locks (package.json, lib/fields.js, tests/test_fields.js) released after this handoff; lib/errors.js was not touched this round so was not re-locked.
RE-VERIFY: replaying npm test (expect 14/14 not 0/0), node --test tests/*.js + quoted native glob, plus independent strict+sloppy mutation probes on frozen FIELDS/displays/Proxy-maps.

VERIFIED rev-2 (verifier-c, node v24.15.0). Both round-1 findings addressed; ACCEPT. (1) FALSE-GREEN DEAD: scripts.test now 'node --test tests/*.js'; REPLAYED from projects/cronsplain/: npm test => tests 14 / pass 14 / fail 0, exit 0 (was 0/0/0). All 3 canonical modes run 14/14 exit 0: npm test, 'node --test tests/*.js', quoted native-glob 'node --test "tests/*.js"'. Bare 'node --test' still reports 0/0 exit 0 (default discovery unchanged) -- ACCEPTABLE since the canonical path (npm test) is now correct; downstream should keep using npm test / explicit glob, never bare 'node --test'. (2) FREEZE HOLDS (independent probe, strict AND sloppy): Object.isFrozen true for FIELDS, each FIELDS entry, MONTH_DISPLAY, DOW_DISPLAY. FIELDS[0].max=999 / FIELDS.push / MONTH_DISPLAY[0]= / DOW_DISPLAY[0]= / MONTH_NAMES.JAN= / new-key MONTH_NAMES.XYZ= / DOW_NAMES.SUN= : in strict mode ALL throw TypeError, in sloppy mode silently no-op, and in BOTH the value is UNCHANGED (max stays 59, length stays 5, January/Sunday/1/0 intact, XYZ stays undefined). The suite's assert.throws(TypeError) is REAL not vacuous -- test_fields.js is 'use strict' and my strict probe confirms the assignments genuinely throw. store frozen pre-Proxy; get-path still resolves case-insensitively (MONTH_NAMES.jan/Jan/DEC=1/1/12, 'sun' in DOW_NAMES=true), Object.keys/entries/values/spread/Object.assign/JSON.stringify all correct. (3) NO REGRESSION: CronParseError instanceof+name+expression+field+message intact (errors.js untouched); cross-cwd require works (/tmp abs + project rel); zero-deps holds (only node:test/node:assert/../lib/fields require'd); all 11 original assertions green within the 14. (4) DISCIPLINE: events show re-claim -> locked EXACTLY package.json+lib/fields.js+tests/test_fields.js (errors.js NOT relocked=untouched) -> npm test goal_reached -> handoff; lock.py status=no active lock files (released); working tree has only the 4 owned cronsplain files. ADVISORY (non-blocking, for T-045/T-050 etc): on the frozen Proxy name-maps, Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor / Object.hasOwn / hasOwnProperty with a NON-exact-case key throws a Proxy-invariant TypeError (case-folding trap over a frozen/non-extensible target); structuredClone(map) also throws (inherent to Proxy, pre-existing). These are LOUD (never silent wrong answers) and OUTSIDE the documented contract -- test membership via 'token in MAP' or read via MAP[token] (both verified case-insensitive), not Object.hasOwn/getOwnPropertyDescriptor. Optional future micro-hardening: getOwnPropertyDescriptor trap could return undefined for non-exact-case keys. T-044 root contract is solid; T-045 unblocked.
